verses.text: 'Abraham believed God, and it was counted unto him for righteousness.' Faith reckoned. He was justified by believing, not by working.
'Abraham believed God, and it was counted unto him for righteousness.' Faith reckoned. He was justified by believing, not by working.
'Abraham believed God, and it was counted unto him for righteousness.' Faith reckoned. He was justified by believing, not by working.
To the worker the reward is debt; to the believer 'his faith is counted for righteousness.' Grace, not wages. Righteousness is received, not earned.
To the worker the reward is debt; to the believer 'his faith is counted for righteousness.' Grace, not wages. Righteousness is received, not earned.
David tells the blessedness of the man to whom God imputes righteousness 'without works.' Blessed pardon. Forgiveness is God's free gift.
David tells the blessedness of the man to whom God imputes righteousness 'without works.' Blessed pardon. Forgiveness is God's free gift.
David tells the blessedness of the man to whom God imputes righteousness 'without works.' Blessed pardon. Forgiveness is God's free gift.
Abraham was counted righteous before circumcision, 'the father of all them that believe.' Faith first. The sign came after the faith it sealed.
Abraham was counted righteous before circumcision, 'the father of all them that believe.' Faith first. The sign came after the faith it sealed.
Abraham was counted righteous before circumcision, 'the father of all them that believe.' Faith first. The sign came after the faith it sealed.
Abraham was counted righteous before circumcision, 'the father of all them that believe.' Faith first. The sign came after the faith it sealed.
The promise was 'through the righteousness of faith,' not the law, which 'worketh wrath.' Not by law. The law condemns; the promise is by faith.
The promise was 'through the righteousness of faith,' not the law, which 'worketh wrath.' Not by law. The law condemns; the promise is by faith.
The promise was 'through the righteousness of faith,' not the law, which 'worketh wrath.' Not by law. The law condemns; the promise is by faith.
It is 'of faith... by grace,' sure to all the seed; God 'quickeneth the dead.' Sure promise. Grace makes the inheritance certain.
It is 'of faith... by grace,' sure to all the seed; God 'quickeneth the dead.' Sure promise. Grace makes the inheritance certain.
'Against hope' Abraham believed, 'strong in faith, giving glory to God'; it was imputed for righteousness. Unwavering faith. He trusted God against all appearance.

'Against hope' Abraham believed, 'strong in faith, giving glory to God'; it was imputed for righteousness. Unwavering faith. He trusted God against all appearance.
It was written for us also; faith is imputed to us who believe on Him that raised Jesus, 'delivered for our offences.' Our righteousness. We are justified by the same faith.
